meta.pageTitle: יסודות · Yessodot — Integrative Psychedelic Clinic, Israel

hero.tagline: Yessodot unites Israel's leading clinicians, researchers, and healing centers to bring psychedelic-assisted care to those conventional medicine has left behind.

crisis.title: When the best available care isn't enough
paragraphs: In the wake of October 7 and ongoing military operations, Israel faces a quiet mental health emergency. A generation of elite, highly trained veterans is sidelined by what clinicians call the Polytrauma Triad: post-traumatic stress disorder, mild traumatic brain injury, and chronic pain.

These conditions are biologically linked — forming a cycle of disability that conventional SSRIs and talk therapy often cannot break. These veterans have received the best care available, and still cannot return to functional life.
The science now points toward a new frontier. Yessodot exists to bring it home.

stat1.label: Remission in PTSD and mTBI symptoms documented by Stanford University researchers studying ibogaine-assisted therapy — often after a single five-day treatment

stat2.label: US veterans treated in Mexico at various ibogaine clinics.

stat3.label: U.S. public-private investment in ibogaine research committed since 2025

atmosphere.quote: Healing happens in connection — at the close of a journey few thought they would take.
atmosphere.imageAlt: Veterans and clinicians embracing at sunset at the close of a treatment retreat

approach.title: Three pillars of transformative care
approach.lead: Yessodot integrates clinical rigor, therapeutic depth, and community support — because lasting healing requires all three working in concert.

pillar1.title: Clinical research
pillar1.body: The world's first Phase 1 and Phase 2a ibogaine clinical trials, conducted at Sheba Medical Center's Sagol Neuroscience Center — building the gold-standard evidence base Israel's veterans deserve.

pillar2.title: Integrative therapy
pillar2.body: Whole-person care at the Yessodot clinic — combining psychedelic-assisted sessions with rigorous preparation, therapeutic integration, and ongoing community support.

pillar3.title: Access and equity
pillar3.body: Ensuring that treatment reaches those who need it most. Through scholarship programs and matched funding partnerships, no veteran's healing should be gated by their ability to pay.

science.title: What the research shows
science.lead: Ibogaine is not a fringe treatment. It is the subject of serious clinical attention at the world's leading institutions — and Israel is positioned to lead what comes next.

point1.title: Stanford University
point1.body: Documented "life-changing" reductions in PTSD, depression, and anxiety among U.S. Special Operations veterans treated with ibogaine — often after a single treatment.

point2.title: University of Texas at Austin
point2.body: Completed a 40-person observational ibogaine study and launched an $18 million dedicated psychedelic research center to advance the science further.

point3.title: U.S. federal investment
point3.body: In 2025, Texas allocated $50 million for ibogaine research, matched by $50 million from a drug development partner — a $100 million public-private commitment to FDA-approved drug development.

point4.title: How it works
point4.body: Ibogaine is a naturally occurring alkaloid from the iboga plant, native to Central Africa. Researchers hypothesize its effects on neuroplasticity may allow the brain to "reset" patterns of trauma, addiction, and chronic pain — particularly when paired with clinical preparation and integration.

evidence.title: Measurable recovery
evidence.lead: In the RESET-VET observational program, veterans were assessed on five validated clinical measures before and after a single ibogaine-assisted treatment. Across the board, symptom scores fell — most dropping below the thresholds that define clinical disorder.
evidence.source: Track 1 preliminary Outcome · Sheba Medical Center · May 2026
evidence.captionBold: RESET-VET: symptom trajectories before and after ibogaine treatment.
evidence.caption: Group means across all five measures — global disability (WHODAS-2.0), PTSD (PCL-5), depression (PHQ-9), anxiety (GAD-7), and post-concussion symptoms (RPQ) — declined from pre-treatment screening to post-treatment follow-up, with most patients falling below established clinical thresholds. Preliminary observational data (n = 7 veterans with post-treatment follow-up).
evidence.chartAlt: RESET-VET symptom trajectories before and after ibogaine treatment across Global Disability, PTSD, Depression, Anxiety, and Post-Concussion Symptoms — group means decline across all five measures

partners.title: A coalition built for impact
partners.lead: Yessodot brings together Israel's foremost medical institutions and global pioneers in psychedelic medicine.

partner1.name: Sheba Medical Center
partner1.description: Home to the Sagol Neuroscience Center and the site of Israel's first formal ibogaine clinical trials. Led by Professor Raquel C. Gardner, MD — a behavioral neurologist trained at Harvard and UCSF with extensive experience treating complex combat trauma at the San Francisco VA Medical Center.

partner2.name: Yessodot
partner2.description: Yessodot conducts clinical ibogaine-assisted therapy research in partnership with Sheba Medical Center focused on treatment-resistant PTSD, mTBI, and related conditions. Our program is built on decades of combined clinical experience and a treatment history spanning thousands of patients, with a safety record maintained through rigorous cardiac screening, medical supervision, and post-treatment protocols.

partner3.name: Yessodot Clinic
partner3.description: An integrative psychedelic clinic offering whole-person care that weaves together medical expertise, therapeutic depth, and community. The heart of Yessodot's healing practice in Israel.

leadership.title: The people behind the work
leadership.lead: Yessodot is led by clinicians, researchers, and advocates who have dedicated their careers to healing at the frontier of medicine.

leader1.name: Arik Metzer
leader1.bio: Arik Metzer is a Tel Aviv-based psychotherapist specializing in trauma and PTSD, following a senior leadership career in high-tech. He leads Yessodot's clinical work, shaping its trauma treatment programs and evidence-informed care for veterans.

leader2.name: Boaz Wachtel
leader2.bio: Boaz Wachtel is a medicalization activist and co-author of the Ibogaine Treatment Manual, with decades of experience advancing patient-centered psychedelic care. He brings advocacy expertise and institutional knowledge to Yessodot's mission of expanding veterans' access to ibogaine treatment.

leader3.name: David Lankri, Ph.D.
leader3.bio: David Lankri is a medicinal chemist and neuropharmacologist at Columbia University, where he synthesizes novel iboga alkaloids and studies how they act on the brain. He provides Yessodot with scientific expertise on the chemistry and pharmacology of ibogaine.

leader4.name: Marissa Feinberg
leader4.bio: Marissa Feinberg is a strategic communications leader specializing in emerging therapies. She leads Yessodot's media strategy and program oversight, building awareness and support for veterans' access to ibogaine treatment.

leader5.name: Yael Levy
leader5.bio: Yael Levy is an operations professional with a background in therapeutic practice. She manages Yessodot's administration, logistics, and participant coordination, creating a structured and supportive experience for veterans in the program.

donate.title: Your gift funds what comes next
donate.lead: Support the world's first clinical trials of ibogaine for IDF veterans — and help build a future where "treatment-resistant" no longer means "without hope."
